figures.caption: Fig. 5. Marginal improvement in pass@1 per repair round on HumanEval. The first repair round (R0 → R1) yields the largest gain for all models.

TABLE VI REPAIR PROMPT ABLATION ON HUMANEVAL. CUMULATIVE PASS@1 (%) AT EACH ROUND FOR UP TO THREE PROMPT STRATEGIES ON FOUR MODELS. SCOUT’S MINIMAL RUN IS UNAVAILABLE (SEE TEXT). DELTAS COMPUTED FROM RAW PROBLEM COUNTS BEFORE ROUNDING. R0 AND SUBSEQUENT ROUNDS MAY VARY SLIGHTLY ACROSS RUNS DUE TO API-LEVEL NON-DETERMINISM; ∆ COMPUTED FROM EACH RUN’S OWN R0 .
Fig. 1. Cumulative pass@1 rate on HumanEval across repair rounds for all seven models. Gemini 2.5 Flash achieves the highest final rate (96.3%), while most gains concentrate in the first two rounds.
Fig. 4. Repair success rate by error type across all models. Assertion errors (logical mistakes) are the hardest to repair.
